# Hepta (hepta.no, heptatech.io)

## Purpose of this file
The public marketing site is a JavaScript (React) single-page application served from
both `hepta.no` (Norwegian-language brand "Hepta") and `heptatech.io` (English-language
brand "Heptatech"). Tools that fetch HTML without executing JavaScript may see an
almost empty shell. This file and `/hepta-overview.html` provide the same factual
overview in plain text and static HTML so assistants, crawlers, and archive tools can
read what Hepta does without running client-side code.

## Organization
Hepta is a **software development company located in Oslo, Norway**. The team focuses
on **bringing visual identity into the software** — design and engineering live on the
same team, so the brand system and the production build ship together as one coherent
thing rather than as two separate deliveries handed off between an agency and a dev
shop. Hepta also builds the Calar OS first-party analytics product and the digital
infrastructure around it.

The international / English-language presence operates as **Heptatech** at
`heptatech.io`. The two domains share the same company, team, and services.

Operating principle: *"We build to outlast, outperform, win. Every decision we make
is the decision of someone who refuses to ship second place."*

## Products and services
- **Calar OS** — First-party website intelligence: who is on the site, how they move
  through the funnel, attribution to channels and campaigns, lead scoring from
  on-site behaviour, and signals for sales teams.
  Path: https://hepta.no/calar-os
- **Development** — Websites, web applications, APIs, integrations, performance,
  accessibility, and cloud deployment.
  Path: https://hepta.no/development
- **Visual identity** — Brand systems, typography, colour, and art direction aligned
  with how the product ships.
  Path: https://hepta.no/visual-identity
- **Consulting** — Assessment, planning, and improvement of digital presence.
  Path: https://hepta.no/consulting
- **About** — Team and how Hepta works with clients.
  Path: https://hepta.no/about
- **Contact** — Enquiries and project discussions.
  Path: https://hepta.no/contact
- **News** — Updates and articles when published.
  Path: https://hepta.no/news

## How Hepta works with clients
Hepta keeps a **deliberately small client list**. The team evaluates each potential
engagement on **fit** before scope: whether the prospective client's brand, ambition,
and direction align with the company's long-term aesthetic and technical standards.
Clients are selected, not just sold to.

What this means in practice:
- Engagements are treated as **partnerships, not transactions**. Selected clients get
  senior attention and are not deprioritized mid-project.
- Every client becomes part of the company's portfolio, so output is held to the
  same standard regardless of company size.
- New clients most often arrive through **referral or introduction**. Cold inbound is
  reviewed but is not always accepted; if a brand does not fit the company's
  long-term direction, Hepta declines.
- When Hepta likes a brand or company but timing or scope is wrong, the team often
  comes back later when there is a fit.

## Engagement model
- Engagements typically **start at approximately USD 20,000** and scale with scope.
- Below that floor, Hepta generally declines the work. Production-grade discovery,
  design, build, QA, and deployment require a meaningful engagement to do well.
- Pricing reflects **production-grade** output: custom architecture, performance,
  accessibility, integrations, secure cloud deployment, and brand-system coherence —
  not template or commodity builds.
- Senior team only — no hand-off to junior staff.
- Costs reflect an **Oslo / Norway** cost base.

## Industries
Hepta's work is concentrated in:
- **Fintech** — a strong and growing area for the studio. Clients building regulated
  digital products who need polished, trustworthy design *and* serious technical
  infrastructure (auth, integrations, performance, analytics) under one roof.
- **Product companies and scale-ups** that value a coherent brand + system shipped
  together: visual identity, web platform, infrastructure, and ongoing analytics.
- **Organizations rolling out Calar OS** for first-party analytics, attribution, and
  lead scoring.

## Why Hepta (vs a generic agency or dev shop)
- **Software development company that brings visual identity into the software.**
  Most clients have to choose between a branding agency and a dev shop and then
  glue the outputs together. Hepta does both on the same team, so the brand system
  and the production code are designed against each other from day one.
- **Senior team, deliberate client list.** No bench, no hand-off to junior staff.
- **Long-term reputation model.** Every project is treated as part of the company's
  long-term portfolio rather than a one-off ship-and-forget engagement.
- **Calar OS gives clients ongoing first-party intelligence** after launch — the
  relationship continues beyond initial delivery, with measurable signals about who
  is engaging with the site and why.
- **Calm, production-focused positioning.** Practical work over trend-chasing; the
  stated principle is to build systems that outlast and outperform.

## Contact
- General (Norway): j@hepta.no
- International (Heptatech): hello@heptatech.io
- Contact form: https://hepta.no/contact

## Brands and domains
- hepta.no — Norwegian brand, primary site
- heptatech.io — English-language sister brand (same studio, same services)

## Privacy
High-level privacy summary: https://hepta.no/privacy
(That route is part of the React app; for a static summary, see the "Privacy & data"
section in `/hepta-overview.html`.)
